paper_title: Isotope Analysis and Paleoenvironmental Reconstruction in the Austral Basin

methods.description: Process for preparing oyster shells for isotope analysis, including cleaning, cutting, and imaging to observe growth patterns and perform isotope analysis.

steps.description: Whole and articulated shells of C.(?) hatcheri and C. orbignyi were sampled and the well-preserved umbonal region of the left valve was normally chosen.
steps.searchable_summary: select and sample well-preserved oyster shells

steps.description: After cleaning, some large shells were covered with epoxy resin in order to strengthen the shell, and then cut along the dorsal-ventral (radial) plane.
steps.searchable_summary: clean and reinforce shells with epoxy resin, then cut along radial plane

steps.description: This surface was polished to observe the internal growth pattern.
steps.searchable_summary: polish shell surface to observe growth patterns

steps.description: Thin sections with the same orientation were made for polarizing and cathodoluminescence (CL) microscopy for three thick shells of each species.
steps.searchable_summary: create thin sections for microscopy analysis

steps.description: Scanning electron microscope (SEM) images were taken for selected shells.
steps.searchable_summary: capture SEM images of selected shells

methods.description: Procedure for analyzing stable oxygen and carbon isotopes in oyster shells to infer paleotemperatures and environmental conditions.

steps.description: Approximately 300 micrograms of carbonate were obtained with a dental drill of 0.5 mm diameter for stable oxygen and carbon isotope analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: extract carbonate samples using dental drill

steps.description: Carbon and oxygen isotope analysis were carried out at the Geochronos Lab at Brasilia University, Brazil.
steps.searchable_summary: perform isotope analysis at Geochronos Lab

steps.description: An aliquot of each sample was reacted with 100% H3PO4 at 72°C for 12 hours in glass vials.
steps.searchable_summary: react samples with H3PO4 for isotope analysis

steps.description: The released CO2 masses were analyzed using a Thermo Fisher DELTA V Plus Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometer and a Gas Bench II analyzer.

In order to evaluate the analytical uncertainties, we analyzed four aliquots of the in-house carbonate reference materials BSB and six aliquots of the in-house carbonate reference GEFA in the same batch of the samples.
